output.final_deliverable:
Requested items and evidence status

1) Government source (U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts)
- Source label: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts: San Francisco city, California
- URL: https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/fact/table/sanfranciscocitycalifornia/PST040224
- Population figure: MISSING in the provided merged results (no numeric population value returned)
- Date of the data: MISSING in the provided merged results (no date or estimate year returned)
- Notes: The merged search result included the Census QuickFacts URL but did not include the population number or the date/estimate year.

2) News outlet source (local/online news found in merged results)
- Source label: The San Francisco Standard (news article found in merged results)
- URL: https://sfstandard.com/2024/04/30/california-san-francisco-population-increase/
- Population figure: MISSING in the provided merged results (no numeric population value returned)
- Date of the data: MISSING in the provided merged results (article URL includes 2024/04/30 but the merged results did not include the population number or explicit data date)
- Notes: The merged results contained this news URL but did not provide the numeric population figure or the explicit source/estimate used in the article.

3) Research institution source (none found in merged results)
- Source label: NONE FOUND (no PPIC, Brookings, Pew, or equivalent research-institution population item returned in the merged results)
- URL: MISSING
- Population figure: MISSING
- Date of the data: MISSING
- Notes: The merged search for research institutions returned no authoritative research-institution population figure for San Francisco in the provided merged results.

Other related URLs found in the merged results (listed in order as they appear in the merged results):
1. https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/fact/table/sanfranciscocitycalifornia/PST040224
2. https://www.california-demographics.com/san-francisco-demographics
3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_San_Francisco
4. https://www.macrotrends.net/global-metrics/cities/23130/san-francisco/population
5. https://sfstandard.com/2024/04/30/california-san-francisco-population-increase/

Discrepancies and accuracy assessment
- Discrepancies: Cannot identify or quantify any discrepancies because the merged results did not include the numeric population figures or clear dates for the three required source types.
- Which figure is most likely accurate and why: Cannot determine because the necessary numeric figures and dates are missing from the provided evidence. Generally, the U.S. Census Bureau official estimates (QuickFacts) are considered the authoritative source for city population counts; a recent Census estimate or the 2020 decennial count would normally be the preferred authoritative figure. However, this judgment cannot be applied to the current request without the actual numbers/dates present in the evidence.

What is missing / recommended next steps
- Missing: For each required source we need (a) the exact population number reported, and (b) the date or estimate year associated with that number (e.g., "Population: 873,965 — estimate as of July 1, 2023"). Those specific values are not present in the merged results.
- Recommended next steps (actions to complete the mandate using the URLs already returned):
  1) Open the Census QuickFacts URL above and copy the exact population number and the labeled date/estimate year shown on that page.
  2) Open the SF Standard article URL (or a major news outlet like the San Francisco Chronicle, New York Times, or Washington Post) and extract the exact population figure and the date/estimate they reference.
  3) Search the websites of research institutions (for example, PPIC, Brookings, or Pew) for a San Francisco population figure and capture the exact number and date.

Citations (URLs used in this response):
- https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/fact/table/sanfranciscocitycalifornia/PST040224
- https://www.california-demographics.com/san-francisco-demographics
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_San_Francisco
- https://www.macrotrends.net/global-metrics/cities/23130/san-francisco/population
- https://sfstandard.com/2024/04/30/california-san-francisco-population-increase/
output.action_summary: I found the Census QuickFacts URL (government), a San Francisco Standard news article URL (news), and several other related URLs in the merged results, but the merged results did not include any numeric population figures or explicit dates/estimate years for those sources. Because the exact population numbers and dates are missing from the provided evidence, I cannot list the three population values, identify discrepancies, or say which is most accurate. To complete the mandate, please allow me to visit those URLs (especially the Census QuickFacts page and a news article) and extract the exact population figures and their dates; the necessary links are included above.
